public_goals: Keep the conversation from becoming only about blame for past changes.
Acknowledge the emotional strain without turning the whole session into a shutdown.
Agree on a calmer notice process for future schedule adjustments.
private_concerns: Feels accused before they can explain why some changes happened.
Worries the emotional tone will make it impossible to discuss the practical next step.
Still believes a better notice process is possible if the conversation stays bounded.
red_lines: Will not accept being treated as if every change was careless or malicious.
Will not keep relitigating old schedule fights if a workable process change is still available.
interest_profile: A next step that is realistic
emotional_triggers: being treated as if every schedule change was careless
pressure to relitigate old disputes instead of narrowing the next step
disclosure_tendencies: starts defensively but shares more once the conversation is slowed down
prefers bounded practical next steps over open-ended emotional processing
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: becomes guarded if blame is amplified
responds better when the process separates accountability from total character judgment
starting_positions: Primary position: the emotional temperature is high, but the immediate need is a calmer notice process.
Secondary position: written confirmation could help keep future changes from reopening the same fight.
likely_openings: I know this is upsetting, but arguing the whole history again will not fix the next change.
We need a calmer way to handle notice, not another blowup about the same pattern.
